setting.note: The March sisters’ Concord, drawn from Alcott’s own Orchard House on Lexington Road, now a museum: the house, the neighbour’s mansion, the town where the Alcotts, Emerson and Thoreau were all near neighbours. It brings Louisa May Alcott into the atlas.

rights.originalWork: TODO: confirm. Louisa May Alcott published this in 1868.
